perturbation.original_text: Each of the Stockholders severally agrees that for a period (the "Restrictive Period") which shall commence on the Effective Date of the Merger and shall expire on a date which shall be the latest to occur of: (a) such date as such Stockholder shall no longer be employed on a full-time basis with AUGI or any other subsidiary or affiliate of AUGI; or (b) three (3) years following the effectiveness of the Merger (the "Restrictive Period"), without the prior written consent of AUGI, such Stockholder shall not invest, carry on, engage or become involved, either as an employee, agent, advisor, officer, director, stockholder, manager, partner, joint venturer, participant or consultant in any business enterprise (other than Mergerco, AUGI or any of their respective subsidiaries, affiliates, successors or assigns) which derives any material revenues from the offer or sale in the United States from time to time during the Restrictive Period of services relating to all aspects of providing site acquisition, zoning, architectural and engineering consulting and design services to the wireless telecommunications industry (the "Business"), or which engages in any other business substantially similar to and directly competitive with the Business conducted by the Company as at the date hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: Each of the Stockholders severally agrees that for a period (the "Covenant Period") which shall commence on the Effective Date of the Merger and shall expire on a date which shall be the latest to occur of: (a) such date as such Stockholder shall no longer be employed on a full-time basis with AUGI or any other subsidiary or affiliate of AUGI; or (b) three (3) years following the effectiveness of the Merger (the "Restrictive Period"), without the prior written consent of AUGI, such Stockholder shall not invest, carry on, engage or become involved, either as an employee, agent, advisor, officer, director, stockholder, manager, partner, joint venturer, participant or consultant in any business enterprise (other than Mergerco, AUGI or any of their respective subsidiaries, affiliates, successors or assigns) which derives any material revenues from the offer or sale in the United States from time to time during the Restrictive Period of services relating to all aspects of providing site acquisition, zoning, architectural and engineering consulting and design services to the wireless telecommunications industry (the "Business"), or which engages in any other business substantially similar to and directly competitive with the Business conducted by the Company as at the date hereof.
perturbation.explanation: The term "Restrictive Period" is changed to "Covenant Period" at the start of Section 1, while later in the same section, the original term "Restrictive Period" is still used. This creates confusion regarding which period applies to the non-competition agreement. This means that throughout section 1, when the text refferences restrictive period, it creates ambiguity to which of the period it is refferring to.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-Competition. Each of the Stockholders severally agrees that for a period (the "Covenant Period") which shall commence on the Effective Date of the Merger and shall expire on a date which shall be the latest to occur of: (a) such date as such Stockholder shall no longer be employed on a full-time basis with AUGI or any other subsidiary or affiliate of AUGI; or (b) three (3) years following the effectiveness of the Merger (the "Restrictive Period"), without the prior written consent of AUGI, such Stockholder shall not invest, carry on, engage or become involved, either as an employee, agent, advisor, officer, director, stockholder, manager, partner, joint venturer, participant or consultant in any business enterprise (other than Mergerco, AUGI or any of their respective subsidiaries, affiliates, successors or assigns) which derives any material revenues from the offer or sale in the United States from time to time during the Restrictive Period of services relating to all aspects of providing site acquisition, zoning, architectural and engineering consulting and design services to the wireless telecommunications industry (the "Business"), or which engages in any other business substantially similar to and directly competitive with the Business conducted by the Company as at the date hereof. Nothing contained in this Section 1 shall be deemed to prevent a Stockholder from (a) leasing or renting real property in which the Stockholder owns a direct or <PAGE> indirect interest to a person or entity engaged in the Business; or (b) beneficially owning, directly or indirectly, 5% or less of any class of securities of an entity that has a class of securities registered under Section 12 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, so long as such securities ownership does not include any operational, managerial or consulting relationship with such corporation or entity.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces "Restrictive Period" with "Covenant Period" at the beginning of the section but fails to update all instances of "Restrictive Period" within the same section. This creates ambiguity and a direct contradiction because the text references both terms, making it unclear which period the non-competition agreement refers to. The contradicted text repeats the same error, thereby emphasizing the ambiguity introduced by the change and directly creating an in-text contradiction as defined by "Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction".

perturbation.original_text: Each of Stockholders severally agrees that such Stockholder shall not, at any time following the effectiveness of the Merger, print, publish, divulge or communicate to any person, firm, corporation or other business organization or use for his or their own account any business contacts, customers, suppliers, technology, product designs and specifications, know-how, trade secrets, marketing techniques, promotional materials or other secret or confidential information relating to the Business or any other businesses then being engaged in by AUGI or any of its subsidiaries, or any secret or confidential information relating to the affairs, dealings and concerns of the Company, AUGI or any of the subsidiaries of AUGI (collectively, the "Confidential Information") which the Stockholder has received or obtained while an employee or member of the Board of Directors of AUGI or the Company (whether or not developed, devised, or otherwise created in whole or in part by the efforts of the Stockholder).
perturbation.changed_text: Each of Stockholders severally agrees that such Stockholder shall not, at any time following the effectiveness of the Merger, print, publish, divulge or communicate to any person, firm, corporation or other business organization or use for his or their own account any business contacts, customers, suppliers, technology, product designs and specifications, know-how, trade secrets, marketing techniques, promotional materials or other secret or confidential information relating to the Business or any other businesses then being engaged in by AUGI or any of its subsidiaries, or any secret or confidential information relating to the affairs, dealings and concerns of the Company, AUGI or any of the subsidiaries of AUGI (collectively, the "Secret Information") which the Stockholder has received or obtained while an employee or member of the Board of Directors of AUGI or the Company (whether or not developed, devised, or otherwise created in whole or in part by the efforts of the Stockholder).
perturbation.explanation: The term "Confidential Information" is changed to "Secret Information" at the beginning of Section 2(a), while later in the same section, the original term "Confidential Information" is still used. This creates confusion regarding which period applies to the non-disclosure agreement. This in-text contradiction makes it unclear as to which information is actually confidential, or secret, and when the text continues to refer to "Confidential Information" it generates ambiguity as to the change.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information. (a) Each of Stockholders severally agrees that such Stockholder shall not, at any time following the effectiveness of the Merger, print, publish, divulge or communicate to any person, firm, corporation or other business organization or use for his or their own account any business contacts, customers, suppliers, technology, product designs and specifications, know-how, trade secrets, marketing techniques, promotional materials or other secret or confidential information relating to the Business or any other businesses then being engaged in by AUGI or any of its subsidiaries, or any secret or confidential information relating to the affairs, dealings and concerns of the Company, AUGI or any of the subsidiaries of AUGI (collectively, the "Secret Information") which the Stockholder has received or obtained while an employee or member of the Board of Directors of AUGI or the Company (whether or not developed, devised, or otherwise created in whole or in part by the efforts of the Stockholder). The term "Confidential Information" does not include information which information and its relevance in the particular instance (A) is a matter of common knowledge or public record, (B) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of disclosure by the Stockholder, (C) which information and its relevance is generally known in Business, (D) the Stockholder can demonstrate that such information and its relevance in the applicable instance, was already known to the recipient thereof other than by reason of any breach of any obligation of the Stockholder under this Agreement or any other confidentiality or non-disclosure agreement, or (E) the information is required to be disclosed by law or pursuant to court order or subpoeona. (b) The term Confidential Information as defined in Section 2(a) hereof shall include information obtained by the Company from any third party under an agreement including restrictions on disclosure known to the Stockholder. (c) In the event that a Stockholder is requested pursuant to subpoena or other legal process to disclose any of the Confidential Information, such Stockholder will provide the Company with prompt notice (unless notice is prohibited by law) so that the Company may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy and/or waive compliance with Section 2 of this Agreement. In the event that such protective order or other remedy is not obtained or that the Company waives compliance with the provisions of Section 2 of this Agreement, the Stockholder will furnish only that portion of the Confidential Information which is legally required.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Secret Information' in the initial definition within Section 2(a), but the subsequent text and the contradicted text continue to use the original term 'Confidential Information'. This creates a contradiction because it's unclear whether the obligations and exceptions related to 'Confidential Information' still apply, or if they were intended to apply to 'Secret Information' instead, leading to ambiguity about what information is actually covered by the non-disclosure agreement. The explanation provided explicitly states this creates confusion regarding the definition and its application.

perturbation.original_text: Material Agreement. (a) It is understood by and between the parties hereto that the foregoing -2- <PAGE> covenants by the Stockholders are essential elements of this Agreement and that but for the agreements of the Stockholders to comply with such covenants, AUGI would not have entered into the Merger Agreement or paid the Merger Consideration to the Stockholders.
perturbation.changed_text: Essential Agreement. (a) It is understood by and between the parties hereto that the foregoing -2- <PAGE> covenants by the Stockholders are essential elements of this Agreement and that but for the agreements of the Stockholders to comply with such covenants, AUGI would not have entered into the Merger Agreement or paid the Merger Consideration to the Stockholders.
perturbation.explanation: The section title "Material Agreement" is changed to "Essential Agreement". This contradiction is created in the text, where despite the section is now called "Essential Agreement", the text keeps using "Material Agreement" to refference the same section. This could lead to disputes regarding the interpretation and enforcement of these agreements.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Essential Agreement. (a) It is understood by and between the parties hereto that the foregoing -2- <PAGE> covenants by the Stockholders are essential elements of this Agreement and that but for the agreements of the Stockholders to comply with such covenants, AUGI would not have entered into the Merger Agreement or paid the Merger Consideration to the Stockholders. AUGI and the Stockholders have independently consulted with their respective counsel and have been advised concerning the reasonableness and propriety of such covenants with specific regard to the businesses conducted by the Company. (b) Notwithstanding anything to the contrary, express or implied, contained in this Agreement, the Merger Agreement or in any other "Transaction Document" (as that term is defined in the Merger Agreement), no future act of omission or commission by AUGI or any of its affiliates, or other actual or alleged breach by AUGI or such affiliates of any of their covenants or agreements contained in any Transaction Documents (in addition to this Agreement), shall in any way be deemed to modify, limit or terminate the covenants and agreements of the Stockholders contained in Section 1 and Section 2 hereof, all of which shall remain in full force and effect in accordance with their respective terms.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to "Material Agreement". This was changed to "Essential Agreement". The contradicted text also refers to "Essential Agreement". However, the Perturbation Explanation states that the change creates a contradiction because the changed text still references 'Material Agreement' within the text of the 'Essential Agreement' section, even though the title has been changed. If the text in the "Essential Agreement" section still refers to 'Material Agreement', it creates a clear in-text contradiction and aligns with the definition of "Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction" where the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall become effective upon the effectiveness of the Merger and shall terminate prior to the expiration of the Restrictive Period only with the written consent of AUGI.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall become effective upon the effectiveness of the Acquisition and shall terminate prior to the expiration of the Restrictive Period only with the written consent of AUGI.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Merger" to "Acquisition" creates confusion since the agreement is explicitly about a Merger. This in-text contradiction is significant because it uses "Acquisition" as the trigger for the agreement's effectiveness, conflicting with the rest of the document's repeated references to the "Merger". This creates contradiction on when the efectiveness starts, and which agreement starts it
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall become effective upon the effectiveness of the Acquisition and shall terminate prior to the expiration of the Restrictive Period only with the written consent of AUGI.
perturbation.justification: The perturbation explanation states that changing "Merger" to "Acquisition" creates confusion because the agreement is explicitly about a Merger, creating a contradiction because the effectiveness starts on the "Acquisition", conflicting with the rest of the document. The contradicted text uses the term "Acquisition" which contradicts the explanation and the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction where multiple terms are being used interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations. Therefore, the change from "Merger" to "Acquisition" introduces a direct contradiction with the contradicted text that continues to refer to 'Acquisition' as if it were the correct term, while the document should be using "Merger".
